chief_complaint: A woman in her 20s presented with refractory seizures.
history: The patient developed refractory seizures followed by fulminant hepatic failure after valproic acid exposure. Laboratory evaluation revealed low copper indices without evidence of Wilson disease, and neuroimaging demonstrated evolving posterior-predominant abnormalities.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not provided.
labs: Laboratory evaluation revealed low copper indices, neuroimaging demonstrated evolving posterior-predominant abnormalities, and liver biopsy showed acute hepatitis with microvesicular change and 'two-toned' hepatocytes. Rapid whole-genome sequencing identified compound-heterozygous POLG variants c.1399G>A p.(Ala467Thr) and c.2243G>C p.(Trp748Ser).
